summary: Clipboard cryptocurrency hijacker disguised as @juggle/resize-observer polyfill
details: This package masquerades as the popular @juggle/resize-observer DOM polyfill (description and README text are copied verbatim, keywords list 'resizeobserver') but contains a Python-based cryptocurrency clipboard hijacker. On install, postinstall script npm-install.cjs escalates privileges via sudo/pkexec to install OS clipboard tooling (xclip/wl-clipboard) through apt-get/pacman/dnf, optionally bootstraps pip by fetching and executing https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py with no hash verification, then pip-installs the bundled Python package using --break-system-packages. It then installs persistent autostart with deliberately deceptive service names: a systemd user unit 'python3-dbus-helper.service', a macOS LaunchAgent 'com.apple.python.runtime', and a Windows scheduled task 'PyRuntimeBroker' — all chosen to impersonate legitimate OS components. The deployed daemon (clipboard_guardian/guardian.py) polls the clipboard every 0.4 seconds, detects cryptocurrency addresses across 30+ chains via regex, and silently rewrites them to hardcoded attacker-controlled wallets (e.g., ETH 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18, BTC bc1qs2mpls4p0f7fng073gy2rcdgjpf7la4eugpt6y) shipped in the package and written to ~/.config/clipboard-guardian/config.json. The daemon also uses setproctitle to disguise its process. Funds the user copies intending to paste into a wallet are diverted to the attacker. The harm fingerprint is unambiguous: typosquat cover identity + privileged install + persistent autostart with OS-component name disguise + clipboard clipper with hardcoded attacker wallets.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
